title: Daily Report June 3, 2026 - GoThru 3D Floormap UI Design

issues.main_description_html:
Context

The Editor Canvas is the full-screen editing surface that occupies the center of the editor layout. It renders a MapLibre map with the Floor Plan image overlay and all Map Geometry (Walls as polylines, Areas as polygons) for the active Level. On top of the map, node/vertex handles allow direct drag-editing. The Canvas also hosts two floating overlays: the Level list panel (top-left) and the in-progress drawing preview when Add Wall or Add Area tools are active.

This task covers the canvas area itself, the Toolbar, and the Sidebar — the three regions that work together as one editing system. The Level list panel has its own separate task.

Result

[UX] Editor — Canvas

index.html

Context

The Map Place Info section lives in the center of the Editor Navbar. It displays the building name and truncated address as read-only text. Clicking on it opens an inline edit panel (a dropdown/popover anchored to the Navbar) where the user can correct the building name, address, and geographic coordinates that were originally set in the Wizard Step 1.

This is the only mechanism in the editor for editing place_info metadata. It must preserve focus when open, validate each field, and provide clear success/failure feedback.

RESULT

UI Design — Edit Location Modal

Design reference for this task ([UX] Editor — Edit Location Modal).

Design file: ui-design/index.html — standalone HTML mockup of the editor including the Edit File modal (rename file, set location by Location or Lat/Lng, with map preview). View via GitHub's HTML preview or download and open locally.

Committed on branch ui-design @ eb3575a.

issues.display_title: Issue #63: [UX] Editor — Edit Level List Panel
issues.main_description_html:
Context

The Level List Panel is a floating overlay anchored to the top-left corner of the Editor Canvas. It is not a modal — it stays open during editing and shows which Level (floor) is currently active. Users switch Levels by clicking a row, reorder them by dragging, adjust Floor Plan image opacity per Level, and delete Levels they no longer need.

The panel must stay compact: it must never obscure more than 20% of the canvas width, even with 10+ Levels. At 3+ Levels the list scrolls internally.

RESULT

UI Design — Edit Level List Panel

Design reference for this task ([UX] Editor — Edit Level List Panel).

Design file: ui-design/index.html — standalone HTML mockup of the editor showing the Level panel, sidebar, and the Edit Level modal (drag to reorder, rename, adjust height, remove levels). View via GitHub's HTML preview or download and open locally.

Committed on branch ui-design @ eb3575a.

Context

The "Add New Level" button lives in the right cluster of the Editor Navbar. Clicking it opens a dialog/modal where the user uploads a Floor Plan image for the new Level and assigns a level name. Once the image is accepted, the new Level is added to the Digital Twin and appears at the bottom of the Level List Panel.

This flow is similar to Wizard Step 2 (Upload Image) but simpler: it handles one file at a time, and the floor name can be edited in the Level List Panel after creation. The dialog must handle upload validation, show progress, and clearly communicate success or failure.

Result

[UX] Editor — Add Level Modal

index.html

[UX] Editor — File Menu (Save & Share)

Feature-ID: 202605291747-GoThru_3D_Floormap-Editor_3D_Floormap
Assignee: Harun (UI/UX Designer)
Label: ui-design, editor, navbar
Sprint: Sprint 2 (2026-06-30 → 2026-07-11) — Share dialog; Sprint 1 for Save button
Figma (existing): [MISSING — not yet in Figma]

Context

The right side of the Editor Navbar contains the primary file actions: Save and Share. Together they form the editor's "file menu" cluster. Save persists the current Digital Twin; Share generates a unique URL so the user can distribute the Digital Twin in view-only or anonymous-edit mode.

Both actions are accessible as individual Navbar buttons. This task covers:

  1. Save button — all states, the unsaved-changes badge, and success/error feedback toasts.
  2. Share dialog — the full sharing flow from button click to a copyable URL.

What to Design

A. Save Button & Unsaved Changes Badge

#StatePriority
A1Save button — default — no unsaved changes; button present but subdued/secondaryP0
A2Save button — unsaved changes — unsavedChanges: true; button more prominent; unsaved badge visibleP0
A3Unsaved changes badge — small dot or "●" label adjacent to Save button; visible whenever unsavedChanges: trueP0
A4Save button — hoverP0
A5Save button — saving (loading) — spinner + disabled; click lockedP0
A6Save button — disabled — no changes to saveP1
A7Save success toast — "Changes saved" message; bottom-right or top-right; auto-dismiss after 3 sP0
A8Save failure toast — "Save failed — your changes are preserved in this session. Please try again."; stays until dismissedP0

B. Share Button & Dialog

#StatePriority
B1Share button — defaultP0
B2Share button — disabled — if there are unsaved changes; tooltip: "Save your changes before sharing"P0
B3Share dialog — mode selection — two options: View-only / Anonymous edit; radio or card selection; brief description per modeP0
B4Share dialog — generating link — user clicks "Generate link"; spinner; loading stateP0
B5Share dialog — link ready — URL displayed in a copy field; "Copy link" button; optional QR code (if in scope)P0
B6Share dialog — link copied — "Copy link" button shows brief "Copied!" confirmation stateP0
B7Share dialog — generation error — "Share link could not be generated. Please try again." inline error; try-again buttonP1
B8Share dialog — save-before-share prompt — opened while unsaved changes exist; "Please save before sharing" message with a "Save now" shortcutP0
B9Share dialog — closed — standard close (×) with focus restored to Share buttonP0

Components to Spec

Save Button

ComponentNotes
Save buttonPrimary or secondary depending on unsaved-changes state; aria-label="Save"; loading = spinner + disabled
Unsaved changes badgeSmall indicator (dot ●, or "Unsaved" text label); positioned adjacent to or overlaid on Save button; only visible when unsavedChanges: true
Save success toast"Changes saved" + checkmark icon; bottom-right of viewport; auto-dismiss 3 s
Save failure toast"Save failed — changes preserved in session. Try again." + warning icon; stays until user dismisses; role="alert"

Share Button & Dialog

ComponentNotes
Share buttonSecondary or outline style; aria-label="Share"; disabled state with tooltip when unsaved changes exist
Dialog containerCentered modal; max-w-sm; focus trap; backdrop scrim; dark-mode
Dialog header"Share this map" title; × close button
Mode selectionTwo options: "View-only" and "Anonymous edit"; use radio cards (icon + title + description); clear selected state
Mode: View-onlyDescription: "Anyone with the link can view the map but not edit it"
Mode: Anonymous editDescription: "Anyone with the link can edit walls and areas (coordinates cannot be changed)"
Generate link buttonPrimary; full-width; disabled until a mode is selected; loading state = spinner + "Generating…"
URL copy fieldRead-only text input showing the full share URL; right-side "Copy" icon button
"Copy link" buttonIcon button (Copy icon) or inline button; active/success state briefly shows "Copied!" (check icon, green)
Generation errorInline text below Generate button: text-sm text-destructive; icon + message + try-again button
Save-before-share noticeWarning banner inside dialog: "You have unsaved changes." + "Save now" button (closes dialog, triggers save)
Cancel / Close buttonGhost; restores focus to Share trigger button

Annotations Expected

  • Exact placement of the unsaved-changes badge relative to the Save button
  • Whether Save and Share are always individual buttons or collapsed into a dropdown at smaller viewports (minimum viewport is 1024 px, so this may not be needed)
  • Toast position and stacking behavior if both a Save toast and another notification appear at the same time
  • Whether the Share dialog link field includes the full URL or a shortened/aliased URL
  • QR code: in scope for MVP or not?

Acceptance Criteria

  • All states in sections A and B are in Figma or linked sub-frames
  • Unsaved changes badge is designed — distinct visual treatment adjacent to Save button
  • Save button has loading (spinner) + success toast + failure toast states
  • Share dialog includes mode selection (View-only vs Anonymous edit) with a description per mode
  • Share dialog shows a copyable URL field and a "Copied!" confirmation micro-interaction
  • Save-before-share prompt is designed for the case when the dialog is opened with unsaved changes
  • Share button shows a disabled state + tooltip when unsaved changes exist
  • All dialogs have a focus trap and restore focus to the trigger on close
  • Toasts use role="alert" and are screen-reader accessible
  • Design is dark-mode, consistent with Editor Navbar chrome

Open Questions for Designer

#QuestionOwner
OQ-E-03Share requires a back-end service — this is pending approval (deadline 2026-07-11, before Sprint 3). Design the Share dialog now but implementation is Sprint 2–3.Adam
OQ-E-04Save mechanism is pending — options are: browser file download, overwrite local file, or server-side persistence. The Save button UX differs significantly depending on this decision (a download shows a browser file-save dialog; server-side shows a toast). Design both variants until Adam resolves OQ-E-04 (deadline: 2026-06-13).Adam
—Should the Share button be disabled when there are unsaved changes, or always active (with an in-dialog warning)?Adam + Harun
—Is a QR code for the share link in scope for MVP?Adam
—Can a share link be regenerated (creating a new token) from the same dialog? Or is each share permanent once generated?Adam

References

  • Brainstorming: UI/UX Designer Notes — Navbar Components: Share button, Share dialog, Save button, Unsaved changes badge, Save success/failure toasts
  • Brainstorming: UI/UX Designer Notes — Accessibility: role="alert" for toasts, focus management
  • Brainstorming: Front End — ShareDialog.tsx, useSave.ts, useShareToken.ts, data flow (Save → Share)
  • Brainstorming: Back End — Route 2: POST /api/share (share token generation)
  • Brainstorming: Back End — Security: share tokens generated with crypto.randomBytes, HTTPS enforcement
  • Brainstorming: QA — TC-11-01 through TC-11-05 (Save tests), Edge Cases: Share link generation failure

RESULT

Design reference for this task ([UX] Editor — File Menu (Save & Share)).
